gaps: No test case covers the scenario of an outdated Charge Case firmware update popup appearing briefly at app launch (on the Home Screen) and then disappearing. All existing tests focus on: (a) language-change-triggered DFU flows, (b) manual navigation to the Software Update tab, or (c) popups that appear after a language change — none test the automatic popup that fires on app launch when the charge case firmware is outdated.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Missing: Testing the popup's behavior across app lifecycle events. The bug is that the popup appears 'briefly' and then vanishes. No test validates: (1) what happens when the app is backgrounded and foregrounded while the popup is showing, (2) what happens when the app is killed and relaunched (does the popup reappear every time?), (3) whether the popup is dismissed automatically or by user interaction, and (4) whether the popup's transient nature causes users to miss the update notification entirely.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Missing: Testing exactly WHEN the popup state is evaluated. The bug implies the popup fires on app launch (Home Screen load) but may not persist or may be overridden by another UI event. No test validates: (1) the timing of the popup relative to Home Screen rendering, (2) whether the popup is suppressed if another modal (e.g., Companion Card, Release Notes) is already showing, (3) whether the popup is a one-time trigger or reappears on subsequent launches until dismissed/actioned.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — Missing: The bug is specific to Voyager Free 60+ (which has a smart charge case with a touchscreen). No test validates this popup behavior specifically for the Free 60+ charge case vs. other Poly devices with charge cases (e.g., Voyager Free 20 which has a non-smart case). The existing tests for 'Case Update Needed' popup (66363596) are tied to language change flows, not to app launch scenarios.

Missing: No test validates the user experience when the popup appears and disappears before the user can interact with it. This is a UX/notification persistence gap — the popup should either remain until dismissed or provide a persistent indicator (badge, banner) on the Home Screen that an update is available for the charge case specifically.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-001: Charge Case Update Popup Appears on App Launch — Voyager Free 60+
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ earbuds are connected to the phone. Charge case firmware is outdated (older version available via Poly Studio Mobile app).
1. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Observe the Home Screen immediately after launch.
4. Note whether a popup appears with text indicating a charge case firmware update is available (e.g., 'Case Update Needed' or similar).
5. Observe whether the popup persists on screen or disappears automatically after a brief period (e.g., 2-5 seconds).
6. If the popup disappears, check the Home Screen for any persistent indicator (badge, banner, icon) that an update is still available.
generated_testcases.expected: A popup indicating a charge case firmware update is available appears on the Home Screen after app launch. The popup should persist until the user explicitly dismisses it (taps 'Update', 'Later', or closes it). The popup must NOT disappear automatically after a brief period. If the popup does auto-dismiss, a persistent visual indicator (e.g., a badge on the Device card or a banner) must remain on the Home Screen to notify the user of the available update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-002: Charge Case Update Popup Reappears After App Background/Foreground Cycle
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the charge case update popup to appear on the Home Screen.
2. Do NOT interact with the popup (do not tap any button).
3. Press the Home button on the phone to background the app.
4. Wait 10 seconds.
5. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list (foreground the app).
6. Observe whether the charge case update popup is still visible or reappears.
7. If the popup is gone, navigate to Device → Software Update and verify the charge case update is still listed.
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding the app, the charge case update popup should still be visible (if it was not dismissed). If the popup was auto-dismissed during the lifecycle transition, the Software Update screen must still show the charge case update as available. The user must not lose the update notification due to a lifecycle event.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-003: Charge Case Update Popup Reappears After App Force-Kill and Relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the charge case update popup to appear on the Home Screen.
2. Do NOT interact with the popup (do not tap any button).
3. Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps or force stop via Settings).
4. Wait 5 seconds.
5. Open Poly Studio Mobile app again.
6. Observe the Home Screen immediately after relaunch.
7. Verify whether the charge case update popup appears again.
8. If the popup appears, tap 'Update' and verify the user is taken to the Software Update screen with the charge case update listed.
9. If the popup does NOT appear, navigate to Device → Software Update and verify the charge case update is still listed.
generated_testcases.expected: After force-killing and relaunching the app, the charge case update popup MUST reappear on the Home Screen, since the update was never actioned or dismissed. The popup should not be a one-time trigger that is lost after app restart. The user must be able to tap 'Update' to proceed to the Software Update screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-004: Charge Case Update Popup Dismissal — 'Later' Action Persists Across App Restart
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the charge case update popup to appear on the Home Screen.
2. Tap 'Later' (or the dismiss/close button) on the popup.
3. Verify the popup is dismissed and the Home Screen is visible.
4. Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
5. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Observe whether the charge case update popup reappears on the Home Screen.
7. Navigate to Device → Software Update and verify the charge case update is still listed as available.
generated_testcases.expected: After tapping 'Later' and force-killing the app, the charge case update popup should NOT reappear on the next launch (since the user explicitly dismissed it). However, the Software Update screen must still show the charge case update as available for manual installation. The 'Later' action should suppress the popup for a reasonable period (e.g., 24 hours or until next reboot) but not remove the update from the Software Update list.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-005: Charge Case Update Popup Suppression When Another Modal Is Already Showing
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Before the charge case update popup appears, trigger another modal to appear on the Home Screen (e.g., Companion Card notification, Release Notes popup, or Bluetooth permission dialog).
3. Observe whether the charge case update popup appears on top of the existing modal, or is suppressed/queued.
4. Dismiss the first modal.
5. Observe whether the charge case update popup now appears, or if it was lost entirely.
6. Navigate to Device → Software Update and verify the charge case update is listed.
generated_testcases.expected: If another modal is already showing when the charge case update popup is triggered, the update popup should be queued and appear immediately after the first modal is dismissed. The update notification must NOT be silently dropped due to modal stacking. The Software Update screen must always show the available charge case update regardless of popup behavior.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-006: Charge Case Update Popup Timing Relative to Home Screen Rendering
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated.
1. Enable screen recording or use a second device to capture the screen of the phone under test.
2. Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and start screen recording immediately.
4. Observe the Home Screen loading sequence frame by frame.
5. Note the exact moment the charge case update popup appears relative to: (a) Home Screen background rendering, (b) Device card rendering, (c) any other UI elements appearing.
6. Measure the duration the popup remains visible before any auto-dismissal (if applicable).
7. If the popup auto-dismisses, note the exact time in seconds from appearance to disappearance.
generated_testcases.expected: The charge case update popup should appear only after the Home Screen has fully rendered (all cards and elements visible). The popup must remain visible for at least 5 seconds if auto-dismissal is implemented, or persist indefinitely until user interaction. The popup must not appear and disappear before the user can reasonably interact with it (minimum 3 seconds of visibility).

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-007: Device Asymmetry — Voyager Free 20 (Non-Smart Case) Does NOT Show Charge Case Update Popup
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 earbuds are connected to the phone. The Free 20 charge case has no touchscreen/smart functionality.
1. Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Observe the Home Screen for any popup indicating a 'Case Update Needed' or charge case firmware update.
4. Navigate to Device → Software Update.
5. Verify whether any charge case firmware update option is listed (it should NOT be, since Free 20 has a non-smart case).
generated_testcases.expected: For Voyager Free 20 (non-smart charge case), no charge case update popup should appear on the Home Screen at app launch. The Software Update screen should NOT list any charge case firmware update option. This confirms the popup is correctly gated to devices with smart charge cases (Voyager Free 60+ only).

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-008: Charge Case Update Popup Persistence During Bluetooth Disconnect/Reconnect
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated. Charge case update popup is visible on the Home Screen.
1. With the charge case update popup visible on the Home Screen, turn off Bluetooth on the phone.
2. Observe whether the popup remains visible or is dismissed.
3. Wait 10 seconds.
4. Turn Bluetooth back on and wait for the Voyager Free 60+ to reconnect.
5. Observe whether the charge case update popup is still visible or reappears after reconnection.
6. If the popup is gone, navigate to Device → Software Update and verify the charge case update is still listed.
generated_testcases.expected: The charge case update popup should persist during a Bluetooth disconnect event (since the update availability was already determined). After Bluetooth reconnection, the popup should still be visible if it was not dismissed. If the popup was auto-dismissed during the disconnect, it must reappear after reconnection. The Software Update screen must always show the available charge case update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-009: Charge Case Update Popup — Verify Update Flow Completes Successfully
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware outdated. Phone battery > 50%. Earbuds in charge case with case battery > 30%.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the charge case update popup to appear on the Home Screen.
2. Tap 'Update' on the popup.
3. Verify the app navigates to the Software Update screen with the charge case firmware update pre-selected or highlighted.
4. Tap 'Update' or 'Install' to begin the charge case firmware update.
5. Wait for the update to complete (do not interrupt).
6. Verify the update completes successfully with a 'Success' or 'Complete' message.
7. Force-kill and relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Verify the charge case update popup does NOT appear (since the update is now current).
9. Navigate to Device → Software Update and verify the charge case firmware version matches the updated version.
generated_testcases.expected: Tapping 'Update' on the charge case update popup should navigate the user to the Software Update screen where the charge case firmware update can be initiated. The update should complete successfully. After the update, the popup must no longer appear on app launch, and the Software Update screen must show the current firmware version.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-010: Charge Case Update Popup — No Popup When Firmware Is Already Current
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected. Charge case firmware is already the latest version (no update available).
1. Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Observe the Home Screen for any charge case update popup.
4. Navigate to Device → Software Update.
5. Verify the Software Update screen shows 'Your software is up to date' or similar message for both earbuds and charge case.
generated_testcases.expected: When the charge case firmware is already current, no charge case update popup should appear on the Home Screen at app launch. The Software Update screen should confirm all firmware is up to date. The popup must only trigger when an actual update is available.
